steps.title: Identify the temporal contrast
steps.content: The first sentence places us in 1889, when the Eiffel Tower's novel design was criticized. The second sentence describes the present reality: the design is now commonplace. The key signal is this movement from past to present.

steps.title: Select the transition to the present
steps.content: ”Nowadays” anchors the second sentence in the present moment and shows how the tower's design has changed status over time—from controversial novelty to widely replicated architecture. The other choices either suggest logical opposition (contrary), conclude the discussion (conclusion), or add supplementary information (furthermore)—none mark the present time. That matches choice D — D is correct.
